# O1 / NETCONF Interface

Enable the O1 interface by setting `o1.enable_ocudu_o1: true` and using `values-o1.yaml` as a base.

The NETCONF server always listens on SSH (port 830 by default). TLS (port 6513) is optional.

## Enabling TLS

```yaml
o1:
  enable_ocudu_o1: true
  netconfServer:
    tls:
      enabled: true
      certSecret: ""  # see below
```

### Option 1 — Auto-generated self-signed certs (dev/test)

Leave `certSecret` empty. The container generates a self-signed CA, server cert, and client cert at startup and stores them at `/etc/netconf-tls` inside the pod.

### Option 2 — Operator-provided certs (production)

Create a Secret with your CA and server cert/key:

```bash
kubectl create secret generic netconf-tls-certs -n <namespace> \
  --from-file=ca.crt=./ca.crt \
  --from-file=server.crt=./server.crt \
  --from-file=server.key=./server.key
```

Then reference it in values:

```yaml
o1:
  netconfServer:
    tls:
      enabled: true
      certSecret: netconf-tls-certs
```

The secret is mounted read-only at `/etc/netconf-tls`. The container uses it as-is without generating new certs.
